marketing.heroSubtitle: Upload a CSV or Excel file. AXIOM automatically cleans, profiles, and analyses it — then explains what matters in plain English with built-in AI chat, predictions, and clustering.
marketing.noCardNote: No credit card. Full Tier 3 features during the trial.
marketing.whyTitle: Analysts spend up to 80% of their time cleaning data. We built the other 80% back.
marketing.whyCleanBody: Trim, dedupe, infer types, impute missing values, and flag outliers as an ordered, reorderable list of toggleable substeps.
marketing.whyStatsBody: Descriptive stats, correlations, and distributions on every column — with currency codes and your preferred number/date formats applied automatically.
marketing.whyMlBody: K-Means risk clustering, RandomForest predictions, enhanced outlier detection — without leaving the analysis page.
marketing.whyAiBodyHtml: Built-in GPT chat that answers questions about your dataset, plus auto-generated reports with recommendations.
marketing.howStep1Body: Drag any CSV or Excel file (up to 200 MB on Tier 3). Encoding and delimiter are detected automatically.
marketing.howStep2Body: Cleaning, statistics, charts, ML, and predictions run on demand — toggleable step by step so you stay in control.
marketing.howStep3Body: Chat with the AI assistant in natural language. Export a polished report when leadership needs an answer.
marketing.ctaTitle: Ready to stop wrangling and start analysing?
marketing.ctaSubtitle: 60 days of full Tier 3 access. No credit card.
marketing.faqQ1: Do I need to install anything?
marketing.faqA1: No. AXIOM runs in any modern browser on Mac, Windows, Linux, ChromeOS, or tablet.
marketing.faqA2: CSV and Excel (.xlsx). Files up to 200 MB and 1,000,000 rows on Tier 3.
marketing.faqA3: Yes — every new account gets 60 days of full Tier 3 access (AI chat, predictions, ML, and exports). No credit card required.
marketing.faqA4: Your uploaded datasets are stored in our managed PostgreSQL database, accessible only to your account. You can delete a dataset at any time.
marketing.faqQ5: Do I need to know SQL or Python?
marketing.faqA5: No. The whole platform is built around clicking, toggling, and asking questions in plain English.
marketing.homeTitle: AXIOM — Intelligent data analytics, no code required
marketing.offerDescription: 60-day free trial with full Tier 3 access

productHome.guidedTitle: What would you like to analyse today?
productHome.guidedSubtitle: Ask a question, attach a file, or pick a suggestion. Every chat remembers your data and follows a clear methodology.
productHome.expertSubtitle: Describe the algorithm, parameters, and columns. Methodology, metrics, and JSON files remain visible to you.
productHome.guidedPlaceholder: Ask anything about your data… (example: predict the next six months of sales.csv)
productHome.expertPlaceholder: Describe the analysis you want (algorithm, parameters, columns)…
productHome.guidedSuggestion1: Show me what's in the last file I uploaded
productHome.guidedSuggestion2: Find anything unusual in my data
productHome.guidedSuggestion3: What were my best months last year?
productHome.guidedSuggestion4: Group my customers into similar segments
productHome.expertSuggestion1: Run STL on monthly_sales and forecast 6 periods ahead
productHome.expertSuggestion2: Drop columns with > 40% nulls, KNN-impute the rest, and profile
productHome.expertSuggestion3: Train an XGBoost regressor with 5-fold CV and report RMSE / MAE
productHome.expertSuggestion4: RFM segmentation with K-Means, k=4, and show cluster centres

auth.signInSubtitle: Sign in to continue your analysis.
auth.resetPasswordSubtitle: Enter the new password for your account.

auth.resetSuccess: Your password has been reset. You're signed in.
auth.invalidCredentials: The email or password you entered is incorrect.
auth.userExists: An account with that email or username already exists.
auth.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 6 characters.
auth.emailInvalid: Please enter a valid email address.
auth.createAccountSubtitle: 60 days of full Tier 3 access. No card required.

upload.guidedSubtitle: Drop a CSV or Excel file below to start analysing it. You can add a quick note about its contents.
upload.expertSubtitle: CSV or Excel up to 200 MB on Tier 3. Column metadata is parsed as soon as the upload completes.
upload.captionPlaceholder: Example: Q3 sales orders from Salesforce
upload.captionHelp: We'll keep this note with the file so you remember what's in it later.
upload.dropAreaGuided: Tap here or drag a CSV / Excel file
upload.dropAreaExpert: Tap to choose or drag a CSV / xlsx file.
upload.noDatasetsSubtitle: Upload a file above to start exploring.

postgres.name: PostgreSQL
postgres.desc: Query a managed Postgres database — already wired into AXIOM.
postgres.detail: AXIOM ships with a Replit-managed Postgres database. You can ask the assistant to read from any of your tables — no extra setup.

mysql.name: MySQL
mysql.desc: Connect to a MySQL or MariaDB instance.
mysql.detail: Add a MYSQL_URL secret in Replit and tell the assistant which tables to read.

mongodb.name: MongoDB
mongodb.desc: Pull collections from MongoDB Atlas or self-hosted.
mongodb.detail: Add a MONGODB_URI secret. The assistant can flatten documents into tabular views for analysis.

snowflake.name: Snowflake
snowflake.detail: Add SNOWFLAKE_ACCOUNT, SNOWFLAKE_USER, and SNOWFLAKE_PASSWORD secrets to enable.

bigquery.name: BigQuery
bigquery.detail: Connect a Google service account via Replit's Google integration to authorize BigQuery reads.

databricks.name: Databricks
databricks.desc: Read tables from a Databricks workspace.
databricks.detail: Add DATABRICKS_HOST and DATABRICKS_TOKEN secrets to enable SQL warehouse queries.

google-sheets.name: Google Sheets
google-sheets.desc: Pull live data from a Google Sheet into a chat.
google-sheets.detail: Use Replit's Google integration to authorise read access, then point the assistant at a sheet URL.

airtable.name: Airtable
airtable.desc: Sync bases and views as datasets.
airtable.detail: Add an AIRTABLE_API_KEY secret and the base ID. Each table becomes a dataset.

notion.name: Notion
notion.desc: Use a Notion database as a structured source.
notion.detail: Connect Notion via Replit's integration; the assistant can read databases and rich text.

stripe.name: Stripe
stripe.detail: Connect Stripe via Replit's integration; the assistant can pull payments, customers, and MRR.

hubspot.name: HubSpot
hubspot.detail: Connect HubSpot via Replit's integration; the assistant can read contacts, deals, and pipelines.

salesforce.name: Salesforce
salesforce.detail: Connect Salesforce via Replit's integration; the assistant can run SOQL on your behalf.

linear.name: Linear
linear.detail: Connect Linear via Replit's integration; the assistant can pull issues, cycles, and trends.

github.name: GitHub
github.detail: Connect GitHub via Replit's integration to analyze contribution, PR throughput, and issues.

slack.name: Slack
slack.detail: Connect Slack via Replit's integration to summarise channels and analyse engagement.

google-analytics.name: Google Analytics
google-analytics.detail: Authorise via Replit's Google integration; the assistant can pull GA4 reports.

csv-upload.name: CSV / Excel upload
csv-upload.desc: Drop a CSV or Excel workbook into a project.
csv-upload.detail: Use the Files page to upload — supported anywhere AXIOM runs, no setup required.

rest-api.name: REST / JSON
rest-api.desc: Pull from any HTTP API into a dataset.
rest-api.detail: Tell the assistant the endpoint and auth header; it will fetch and tabulate the response.

connectors.title: Data connectors
connectors.subtitle: Bring your data into AXIOM from any source. Some sources are pre-configured, others connect via Replit's integration system — tap a card to see how.

projects.title: Your projects
projects.subtitle: Each project keeps its own data and chat history. Hover a card to reveal rename, archive, and delete. Use the checkboxes to act on multiple projects at once.
projects.noProjectsActive: No projects yet. Create one above to get started.
projects.deleteConfirmBody: Type the project name to confirm deletion. This cannot be undone.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage your account preferences.
settings.languageHelp: Choose the language used across the app. Your selection is saved to your profile.
settings.saveFailed: We couldn't save your preferences. Please try again.
settings.modeHelp: Guided keeps things friendly. Expert shows full technical detail.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
errors.network: We couldn't reach the server. Check your connection and try again.
errors.unauthorized: Your session has expired. Please sign in again.
errors.forbidden: You don't have access to that.
errors.notFound: We couldn't find what you were looking for.
errors.validation: Please check the highlighted fields and try again.

recommendations.title: Recommendations
recommendations.needProject: Recommendations need an active project. Open one from the projects page first.

prediction.confidenceMedium: Medium confidence — review the results before relying on them.
prediction.confidenceLow: Low confidence — we don't recommend trusting this prediction as-is.
prediction.confidenceDescription: We derive the confidence score from how well the model fits (R²). It's measured on a 0–100 scale, where ≥ 70 is high, 40–69 medium, and below 40 low.

dashboard.guidedSubtitle: Auto-suggested KPIs, trends, and segments. Use the filters at the top to narrow every tile at once.
dashboard.expertSubtitle: Saved tile specs on top of the central aggregation engine. Page filters flow through the same SUM/AVG used in pivot, visualize, and chat.
dashboard.duplicateRowsWarn: We couldn't find a unique grain for this table — it has {count} duplicate rows. Aggregations may double-count without a clean primary key.
dashboard.noTilesYet: No tiles yet. Use the {pivotLink} or {visualizeLink} pages to build, or click "Reset" to suggest tiles automatically.
dashboard.kpiHintPercent: Percentage of {label} across rows in range.
dashboard.kpiHintCount: Number of {label} across rows in range.
dashboard.kpiHintGeneric: Aggregated value of {label} across rows in range.
dashboard.kpiHintUncomputable: We couldn't compute {label} for the current range.

chat.greetingNew: Welcome — ask me about any dataset in this project and I will analyse it with you step by step. I can build charts, predictions, statistical analysis, and clustering directly from the conversation.
chat.greetingNoData: This project does not have any data yet. Upload a CSV or Excel file from the side panel and I will start analysing immediately.
chat.attachTitle: Upload a CSV or Excel file
chat.openOutputsTitle: Show outputs panel (charts, tables, model results)
chat.closeOutputsTitle: Hide outputs panel (charts, tables, model results)
chat.finalReportTitle: Open the final report for this chat
chat.guidedPredictTitle: Let's predict from your data in three simple steps.
chat.guidedPredictBody: We scan your columns, ask a few clarifying questions, then show the result with a clear explanation and a confidence score.

footer.tagline: An intelligent data analytics platform that turns raw datasets into clear, actionable insights — in seconds, no code required.

pricing.title: Start free. Stay free if you want.
pricing.leadAfter: — predictions, AI chat, and exports included. No card on file.
pricing.tier1Summary: For anyone who wants to try the cleaning + statistics workflow.
pricing.tier2Summary: For analysts who need the full transform & visualisation toolkit.
pricing.tier3Summary: Predictive ML, AI chat, and reports for teams that ship insight to leadership.
pricing.faq1Q: Do I really get Tier 3 free for 60 days?
pricing.faq1A: Yes. Every new account is granted Tier 3 capabilities for 60 days, no credit card required. After day 60, you choose a plan or downgrade to Tier 1 (free).
pricing.faq2Q: Can I downgrade or cancel anytime?
pricing.faq2A: Yes. Your data stays accessible at the lower-tier limits; nothing is deleted.
pricing.faq3Q: Is there a discount for students or non-profits?
pricing.faq3A: Yes — please reach out to support and tell us about your work.
pricing.faq4A: All datasets and accounts are stored in our managed PostgreSQL database. You can delete a dataset or your entire account at any time.
pricing.metaDescription: Three transparent tiers, every account starts with 60 days of full Tier 3 access. No credit card required.

contact.title: Talk to us.
contact.lead: Support, feedback, or partnership — send a message and we’ll reply within one business day.
contact.successInline: Thanks — we’ll get back to you shortly.
contact.validationError: Please fill in your name, email, and a short message.
contact.fallbackError: Could not send — please try again.
contact.metaDescription: Get in touch with the AXIOM team for support, partnerships, or feedback.

appShell.modeExpertTitle: Full control with technical answers, JSON and metrics

features.metaTitle: Features — every analysis tool, one workspace
features.metaDescription: Auto cleaning pipeline, descriptive stats, visualizations, ML & K-Means clustering, predictions, and an AI assistant — all in one no-code workspace.
features.title: Every analysis tool, one workspace.
features.lead: AXIOM bundles cleaning, transformation, statistics, visualizations, predictions, ML, and an AI chat assistant — all on the same project context, all reachable in two clicks.
features.item1P: Trim, dedupe, infer types, impute missing, and flag outliers as ordered, toggleable substeps with per-step impact metrics. Save as a reusable recipe.
features.item2P: Power Query–style: add column from examples, merge, split, replace, conditional column, group by — all reorderable in the Applied Steps editor.
features.item3P: Mean, median, mode, std, variance, skew, kurtosis, missing %, with currency codes and your number/date format applied automatically.
features.item4P: Find strong correlations and distribution shape across every numeric column in seconds.
features.item5P: Bar, scatter, box, pie, line, heatmap — built with restrained, on-brand palettes. No 3D pie charts.
features.item6P: Save snapshots of your dataset and diff Q1 vs Q2 (or any range) on every column.
features.item7P: Linear models, trend analysis, growth metrics, CAGR — explained in plain English alongside the numbers.
features.item8P: K-Means risk clustering, RandomForest predictions, enhanced outlier detection, categorical insights.
features.item9P: GPT-powered chat that knows your dataset. Ask questions, drive cleaning, generate reports — in any language.
features.item10P: Auto-generated executive summary with key findings, recommendations, and methodological caveats. PDF download.
features.item11P: Group datasets, chats, and saved analyses by project. Each project keeps its own context — no spillover.
features.item12P: Guided Mode for non-specialists (chat-first). Expert Mode for the full dashboard with chat side-panel. Mode preference is per project.

about.metaTitle: About — building the analyst's missing copilot
about.metaDescription: AXIOM is an analyst-built workspace that automates the boring 80% of data work and frees the human for the part that actually moves the business.
about.title: Built by analysts who got tired of the cleanup tax.
about.p1: AXIOM started as one person's frustration with the gap between "here is a CSV" and "here is what to do about it." That gap is usually filled with hours of cleaning, formatting, googling, and Excel formulas — and once it's done, the actual analytical thinking takes ten minutes.
about.p2: So we built the workspace we wanted: one place that ingests any CSV or Excel, runs a transparent cleaning pipeline you can audit step by step, computes the statistics that matter, draws restrained charts that don't look like a 2003 BI dashboard, runs ML when you need it, and lets you ask the data questions in plain English.
about.who1Body: clean, transform, profile, and ship findings without leaving the browser.
about.who2Body: get answers from your data without booking a meeting with the data team.
about.who3Body: focus on the question, not the cleanup.
about.principle1Body: Every cleaning step, every transform, every ML decision is visible and toggleable.
about.principle2Body: If a number can be explained in a sentence, it should be.
about.principle3Body: If a click takes more than a second, we treat it as a bug.

glossary.metaDescription: Plain-English definitions for the terms data analysts actually use, with stats, FAQs, and how-to context.
glossary.indexLead: Plain-English explanations of the concepts that show up in every analyst's job — with current stats, links to deeper guides, and how AXIOM handles them.
glossary.translationInProgress: Arabic translation in progress — the detailed body is shown in English for now.

ab-testing.summary: A controlled experiment that randomly splits users between two versions and measures which produces a better outcome on a chosen metric.

anomaly-detection.summary: Identifying observations or patterns that do not conform to expected behaviour, typically signalling fraud, faults, or genuinely new events.

data-cleaning.summary: The process of detecting and correcting errors, inconsistencies, missing values, and duplicates in a dataset before analysis.

data-drift.summary: A change over time in the statistical properties of input data that degrades a model's accuracy.

descriptive-statistics.summary: Summary measures (mean, median, std, percentiles) that describe the central tendency and spread of a dataset.

etl-vs-elt.summary: Two patterns for moving data into a warehouse — ETL transforms before loading, ELT loads first and transforms inside the warehouse.

k-means-clustering.summary: An unsupervised algorithm that groups observations into K clusters by minimising within-cluster variance.

missing-value-imputation.summary: Filling in missing data with estimated values so downstream analysis and models can run without dropping rows.

normalization.summary: Rescaling numeric features onto a common scale (typically 0–1 or mean 0 / std 1) so they contribute comparably to a model.

outlier-detection.summary: Statistical techniques for finding observations that lie an abnormal distance from the rest of a dataset.

predictive-analytics.summary: Using historical data and statistical or ML models to forecast future outcomes or behaviours.

time-series.summary: Statistical methods for analysing data points indexed in time order to find trend, seasonality, and forecast future values.

guides.metaDescription: Step-by-step walkthroughs for cleaning, analysing, and acting on data with AXIOM.
guides.indexLead: Practical walkthroughs that take you from a messy spreadsheet to an actionable answer — using AXIOM's built-in cleaning, statistics, and AI assistant.
guides.translationInProgress: Arabic translation in progress — the detailed walkthrough is shown in English for now.

how-to-ab-test-a-pricing-change.title: How to A/B test a pricing change
how-to-ab-test-a-pricing-change.summary: Step-by-step: design, size, and analyse an A/B test on a pricing change without statistical bait-and-switch. Includes power analysis, sample size, and how to read the result honestly.

how-to-build-a-3-month-sales-forecast.title: How to build a 3-month sales forecast (no code)
how-to-build-a-3-month-sales-forecast.summary: Step-by-step: turn 24 months of sales history into a defensible 3-month forecast using AXIOM's predictions tab — with honest accuracy bounds.

how-to-clean-a-messy-csv-in-60-seconds.title: How to clean a messy CSV in 60 seconds
how-to-clean-a-messy-csv-in-60-seconds.summary: A 60-second walkthrough for turning a messy CSV — duplicates, missing values, mixed types, stray whitespace — into a clean, analysis-ready dataset using AXIOM.

how-to-compare-this-quarter-vs-last-quar.title: How to compare this quarter vs last quarter (without lying with averages)
how-to-compare-this-quarter-vs-last-quar.summary: A practical guide to a defensible quarter-over-quarter comparison — same-day windows, mix-shift checks, and confidence intervals — using AXIOM's time-period view.

how-to-detect-outliers-in-sales-data.title: How to detect outliers in sales data
how-to-detect-outliers-in-sales-data.summary: A practical guide to spotting outliers in sales data using IQR, z-score, and K-Means clustering — without writing code.
